questions.question: A λ/2 microstrip resonator has an electrical length of:

explanations.a: A λ/8 length is too short for a half-wave resonator.
explanations.b: A λ/4 length corresponds to a quarter-wave resonator.
explanations.c: Correct! A λ/2 microstrip resonator has an electrical length of half the guided wavelength.
explanations.d: A full wavelength is not used for a λ/2 resonator.

questions.question: The resonant length of a λ/2 microstrip resonator is approximately:

explanations.a: The resonator length is based on the guided wavelength, not the free-space wavelength.
explanations.b: Correct! The resonant length is approximately half of the guided wavelength (λg/2).
explanations.c: λ₀/4 corresponds to a quarter-wave length in free space.
explanations.d: A full guided wavelength is not required for a λ/2 resonator.

questions.question: The resonant frequency of a λ/2 resonator is given by:

explanations.a: This expression does not represent the resonant frequency of a half-wave resonator.
explanations.b: Correct! The resonant frequency is given by f = vp/(2L), where vp is the phase velocity and L is the resonator length.
explanations.c: This equation overestimates the resonant frequency.
explanations.d: Frequency is not obtained by multiplying phase velocity and length.

questions.question: In a microstrip resonator, the guided wavelength depends on:

explanations.a: The guided wavelength is independent of the source voltage.
explanations.b: Correct! The guided wavelength depends on the effective dielectric constant of the substrate.
explanations.c: Load resistance does not determine the guided wavelength.
explanations.d: Strip thickness alone does not determine the guided wavelength.

answers.d: Always greater than substrate dielectric constant

explanations.a: An effective dielectric constant of 1 applies only to air.
explanations.b: It is generally lower than the substrate dielectric constant because some fields exist in air.
explanations.c: Correct! The effective dielectric constant lies between 1 and the substrate dielectric constant.
explanations.d: The effective dielectric constant can never exceed the substrate dielectric constant.
